decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Following the optimization history suggestion to try a coordinated multi-variable change, I will simultaneously increase the heat spreader's conductivity and its width. This combines material enhancement with geometric expansion to improve lateral heat spreading more aggressively than single-parameter tuning.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader conductivity within bounds to enhance lateral heat diffusion away from the chip hotspot.
Increasing the spreader width to cover more of the domain, providing a larger cross-section for heat to travel towards the cold boundaries.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
Improved thermal resistance path from chip to side walls
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
Increasing width to 1.0m approaches the design domain limit (1.2m), leaving less margin for future geometric adjustments.
Coordinated changes make it harder to isolate the individual contribution of each variable if further tuning is needed.
